message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>SARS-CoV-2-specific humoral response was analyzed over time in a group of healthcare workers with or without exposure to SARS-CoV-2, who underwent vaccination with BBIBP-CorV (Sinopharm) vaccine in Argentina.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  Seroconversion rates in unexposed subjects after the first and second doses were 40% and 100%, respectively, showing a significant increase in antibody concentrations from dose 1 to dose 2 (
                  <jats:italic>p</jats:italic>
                  <0.0001).
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  The highest antibody concentrations were found in younger subjects and women, remaining significantly associated in a multivariable linear regression model (
                  <jats:italic>p</jats:italic>
                  =0.005).
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  A single dose of the BBIBP-CorV vaccine induced a strong antibody response in individuals with prior SARS-CoV-2infection, while a second dose did not increase this response. A sharp increase in antibody concentrations was observed following SARS-CoV-2 infection in those participants who became infected after the first and second doses (
                  <jats:italic>p</jats:italic>
                  =0.008).
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  Individuals with SARS-CoV-2 exposure prior to vaccination showed significantly higher anti-spike IgG antibody levels, at all-time points, than those not exposed (
                  <jats:italic>p</jats:italic>
                  <0.001). Higher antibody titers were induced by a single dose in previously SARS-CoV-2 infected individuals than those induced in naïve subjects by two doses of the vaccine (
                  <jats:italic>p</jats:italic>
                  <0.0001). Three months after the second dose both groups showed a decline in antibody levels, being more abrupt in unexposed subjects.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>Overall, our results showed a trend towards lower antibody concentrations over time following BBIBP-CorV vaccination. Sex and age seem to influence the magnitude of the humoral response in unexposed subjects while the combination of exposure to SARS-CoV-2 plus vaccination, whatever the sequence of the events was, produced a sharp increase in antibody levels.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>Evaluation of the humoral responses over time and the analysis of the induction and persistence of memory B and T cell responses, are needed to assess long-term immune protection induced by BBIBP-CorV vaccine.</jats:p>
title: Humoral response to the BBIBP-CorV vaccine over time in healthcare workers with or without exposure to SARS-CoV-2

reference.article-title: Effect of 2 Inactivated SARS-CoV-2 Vaccines on Symptomatic COVID-19 Infection in Adults: A Randomized Clinical Trial
